rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirmirming Certain Assets Are Trust Property
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) The 16061.7 Notice was dated with month and day, but not the year. 2) Court to determine whether venue is proper in San Francisco. 3) Petitioner has cited Heggstad and Kucker but has failed to cite any authority regarding confirmation of real property by way of general assignment. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANTON BAJJALIEH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine how to proceed. 2) Issues in Michelle Bajjalieh Petition of Lost Will: [Same issues since 4/21/2021.] a. A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect California creditors, even if the will waives, or all beneficiaries waive, bond. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000. [LRSF 14.16 F.] In response attorney E. Neal McGettigan filed a Declaration on 05/10/2021 that is not signed or verified by Petitioner Michelle Bajjalieh. [See CRC 7.103] b. Petitioner Michelle Bajjalieh has not submitted a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [See LRSF 14.7]. 3) Issues in First Amended Petition for Letters of Administration [Filed 08/11/2021 by Jacqueline Ahwal] a. No objections filed to the 8/11/2021 First Amended Petition for Letters of Administration filed by Jacqueline Ahwal. It appears Michelle Bajjalieh did not receive notice of the petition the DE-121 shows notice was given to her attorney. It appears this was done for 4 individuals Matthew Joseph Bajjalieh, Terah Bajjalieh, Michelle Bajjalieh, and Debra Dolch. Notice needs to be given to each individual entitled to notice not c/o their attorney. [Probate Code 1202]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MUI TAI VONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/17/2021. 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) No DE-147 form is on file. 3) No DE-147S form is on file. This form must be conventionally filed with a pleading cover sheet and cannot be e-filed. 4) The proof of publication shows the first publication was made less than 15 days before the first hearing date. Notice must be republished with the continued hearing date. 5) Item 2d of the petition must be completed (at least one box must be checked). 6) Re item 3f(2): The will is not self-proving as it was not witnessed. Probate Code section 6110(c)(2) must be addressed. 7) Item 3h(3) or (4) must be checked. 8) Re item 8: Is Jimmy Cam's street number accurate? The proof of service shows 919 instead of 1919. 9) If the attorney represented the decedent, is the attorney ethically allowed to represent the petitioners asserting adverse interests as creditors against the decedent's estate? 10) The objection filed on 08/20/2021 was not verified. Also, no Exhibits were attached. No courtesy copy has been received as of 10/04/2021. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MUI TAI VONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/17/2021. 1) No courtesy copy of the petition has been received. 2) No proposed order has been received. 3) The petition caption does not request IAEA authority. 4) No DE-147 form is on file. 5) No DE-147S form is on file. This form must be conventionally filed with a pleading cover sheet and cannot be e-filed. 7) No proof of publication is on file. 6) No DE-121 form is on file. 7) Item 5a(3) or (4) must be checked. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
